perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection,and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential." The term "Evaluation Materials" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by you or your representatives, (ii) was or becomes available to you on a non-confidential BASIS from a source other than the Company or its representatives, provided that such source is not prohibited from disclosing such information to you by a contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation to the Company or its representatives, or (iii) is independently developed by you.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection,and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential."
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exceptions to the definition of 'Evaluation Materials,' the contract creates an in-text contradiction. The contract later refers to the 'Evaluation Materials' without acknowledging exceptions. For example, information that becomes publicly available is now considered confidential, creating confusion and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will not use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. You and your representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that (i) any of such information may only be disclosed to those of your directors, officers, employees, agents, <PAGE> representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "your representatives") who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between you and the Company (it being understood that your representatives shall be informed by you of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by you, and shall each expressly agree, to treat such information confidentially in accordance with this Agreement) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents by writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generosity of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither you nor your representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose. You will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your representatives. In the event that you or any of your representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, you or your representatives as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist, or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Company waives compliance with the provisions hereof, (i) you or your representatives, as the case may be, may disclose to any tribunal only that portion of the Evaluation Materials which you are advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and shall exercise your best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Materials and (ii) you shall not be liable for such disclosure unless disclosure to any such tribunal was caused by or resulted from a previous disclosure by you or your representatives not permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Evaluation Materials' with specific exceptions, such as information that becomes publicly available. The changed text removes these exceptions, making the definition of 'Evaluation Materials' broader. The contradicted text requires the 'Evaluation Materials' to be kept confidential. Since the definition of 'Evaluation Materials' now includes information that is publicly available, the requirement to keep it confidential creates a direct contradiction. Publicly available information cannot be kept confidential, hence creating a strong contradiction between the changed and contradicted sections.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such material, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company's request, you shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such material, and shall certify the destruction of such materials in writing to the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the reference to 'Evaluation Materials' in this clause, the contract becomes unclear as to what materials must be returned or destroyed. Since the obligation is to return 'all written materials' this is contradictory to the definition provided in the previous section that Evaluation Materials does not include all documents provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Evaluation Materials" includes information furnished to you orally or in writing (whatever the form or storage medium) or gathered by inspection,and regardless of whether such information is specifically identified as "confidential."
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific term 'Evaluation Materials' and replaces it with 'all written materials.' The contradicted text defines 'Evaluation Materials' as a specific subset of information. By broadening the scope of what must be returned or destroyed to 'all written materials,' the changed text now contradicts the definition of 'Evaluation Materials,' as it implies that all written materials, not just evaluation materials, are subject to the return/destruction clause. This creates a contradiction because the parties agreed on a set of materials that were to be considered 'Evaluation Materials'. Based on the Perturbation explanation, this falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: During the course of your evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to CSFB or employees or representatives of the Company specified by CSFB. Accordingly, you agree not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate with any executive or other employee of the Company concerning is Possible Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person, without the express consent of CSFB.
perturbation.changed_text: During the course of your evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to CSFB or employees or representatives of the Company specified by CSFB. Accordingly, you agree not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate with any employee of the Company, without the express consent of CSFB.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'concerning is Possible Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person', the clause now prohibits contact with *any* employee of the company for *any* reason. This contradicts the general nature of the agreement, which anticipates ongoing contact for evaluation purposes. The earlier clause, about representatives, implies interaction with employees, but now all is cut off.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Accordingly, you agree not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate with any employee of the Company, without the express consent of CSFB. You also agree not to discuss with or offer to any third party an equity participation in a Possible Transaction or any other form of joint acquisition by you and such third party without CSFB's prior written consent. Without the Company's prior consent, neither your or anybody acting on your behalf shall directly or indirectly for a period of two years from the date hereof (a) induce or encourage any employee of Zeigler to leave employment with Zeigler or (b) employ or hire the services of any executive, managerial, supervisory, technical, or geological employee, of Zeigler, provided that you shall not be prohibited by clause (b) above from employing or hiring the services of any person who has to be an employee of Zeigler for a period of at least 180 days prior to any direct or indirect communication of any kind between such person and you relating to possible or actual employment of such Person or hiring such Person's services.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted communication with employees 'concerning is Possible Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person'. The changed text removes this restriction, now prohibiting *any* communication with employees without CSFB's consent. The contradicted text, which is the same clause after the omission, states the broad restriction on communication with any employee. The contradiction arises because the broader context of the agreement anticipates communication with employees for evaluation purposes as implied by the reference to representatives. The original version allowed such communication related to the transaction, but the changed version forbids it entirely, creating a direct conflict. This fits the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with other sections.

perturbation.original_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors (I) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any Of to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13( )( ) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1834) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger. Except as provided above, you also agree during such <PAGE> period not to request the Company (or its directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives) to amend or waive any provision of this paragraph.
perturbation.changed_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors (I) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any Of to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13( )( ) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1834) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the sentence 'Except as provided above, you also agree during such <PAGE> period not to request the Company (or its directors, officers, employees, agents or representatives) to amend or waive any provision of this paragraph.', the contract now allows the acquirer to request amendments or waivers to the standstill provision. This directly contradicts the purpose of the standstill agreement, which is to prevent the acquirer from taking certain actions. The changed text is contradictory to what a standstill agreement enforces.
perturbation.contradicted_text: STANDSTILL You agree that until two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not without the prior approval of the Board of Directors (I) acquire or make any proposal to acquire any securities or property of the Company, (ii) propose to enter into any merger or business combination involving the Company or purchase a material portion of the assets of the Company, (iii) make or participate in any Of to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of any securities of the Company, (iv) form, join or participate in a "group" (within the meaning of Section 13( )( ) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1834) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, (v) otherwise act or seek to control or influence the management Board of Directors or policies of the Company, (vi) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (vii) take any action which might require the Company to make a public announcement regarding the possibility of a business combination or merger.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented the acquirer from requesting amendments or waivers to the standstill provision. The changed text omits this restriction, thus allowing the acquirer to request amendments or waivers. This directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of the standstill agreement, as it weakens the restrictions intended to prevent certain actions. The Contradicted text has the same text as the changed text, but this doesn't prevent the contradiction from arising. The omission of the sentence in the Changed Text creates a contradiction in light of the original intent and function of the standstill agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Although the Company and CSFB have endeavored to included in the Evaluation Materials Information known to them which they believe to be relevant for the purpose of your investigation, you acknowledge and agree that non of the Company, CSFB or any of the Company's other representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials, and riches of the Company, CSFB or any of the Company's other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to you of any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any of your representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Although the Company and CSFB have endeavored to included in the Evaluation Materials Information known to them which they believe to be relevant for the purpose of your investigation, you acknowledge and agree that non of the Company, CSFB or any of the Company's other representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting ', and riches of the Company, CSFB or any of the Company's other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to you of any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any other person resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by you or any of your representatives.', the contract now implies that liability could be imposed on CSFB for use of information within Evaluation Materials. Removing the liability section, contradicts with 'the information is for your investigation'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Although the Company and CSFB have endeavored to included in the Evaluation Materials Information known to them which they believe to be relevant for the purpose of your investigation, you acknowledge and agree that non of the Company, CSFB or any of the Company's other representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Company and CSFB have no liability for the use of evaluation materials. The changed text omits this liability waiver, now implying that CSFB could potentially be held liable. This directly contradicts the purpose of the evaluation materials mentioned in the contradicted text 'for your investigation' as liability would be a concern if the materials are used for investigation.

perturbation.original_text: You also agree that unless and until a Sale Agreement between the Company and you with respect to the acquisition of the Company has been executed and delivered by you and each of the other parties thereto, there shall not be any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any such transaction by virtue of this agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such transaction except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: You also agree that unless and until a Sale Agreement between the Company and you with respect to the acquisition of the Company has been executed and delivered by you and each of the other parties thereto, there shall not be any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to any such transaction by virtue of this agreement or any other written or oral expression with respect to such transaction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein.', the contract removes the enforceable clauses listed here. This is an issue for things such as NDA
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of our furnishing you with the Evaluation Materials (as defined below) you agree as follows: CONFIDENTIALITY OF EVALUATION MATERIALS You will treat confidentially any information (whether written or oral) that either we or our financial advisor, CREDIT SUISSE FIRST BOSTON CORPORATION ("CSFB"), or our other representatives furnish to you in connection with a Possible Transaction involving the Company, together with analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by you, or by your representatives (as defined below) which contain or otherwise reflect such information or your review of, or interest in the Company (collectively, the "Evaluation Materials"). You recognize and acknowledge the competitive value of the Evaluation Materials and the damage that could result to the Company if the Evaluation Materials were used or disclosed except as authorized by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly carves out exceptions to the 'no legal obligation' clause, preserving enforceability for matters agreed upon in the agreement itself. By removing 'except, in the case of this Agreement, for the matters specifically agreed to herein', the changed text eliminates this carve-out. This directly contradicts the 'Confidentiality of Evaluation Materials' section, which relies on the agreement's enforceability to protect confidential information. The change removes the legal basis for the confidentiality agreement, creating a direct conflict as the changed text states that there is no legal obligation.
